description: Add opentabs plugin install, remove, and list CLI commands. Unify npm vs bun guidance to consistently use npm.

userStories.title: Unify package manager guidance to npm
userStories.description: As a user, I want consistent guidance on which package manager to use for plugin management so that I am not confused by conflicting instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: version-check.ts updateCommand uses 'npm update <pkg>' instead of 'bun update <pkg>'
OnboardingState.tsx install example uses 'npm install -g opentabs-plugin-slack'
No occurrence of 'bun update' or 'bun install -g' in user-facing strings across the codebase (version-check, side panel, CLI output)
userStories.notes:
Three files need changes:
1. platform/mcp-server/src/version-check.ts:102 — change `updateCommand: \`bun update ${pkgName}\`` to `npm update -g ${pkgName}`
2. platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/OnboardingState.tsx:34 — already says npm, verify it stays
3. platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts:70 — already says npm, verify it stays

The key change is in version-check.ts. Search the entire codebase for 'bun update' and 'bun install -g' to catch any other occurrences.

userStories.title: Add opentabs plugin install command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run 'opentabs plugin install <name>' to install a plugin without leaving the CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'opentabs plugin install opentabs-plugin-slack' executes 'npm install -g opentabs-plugin-slack' as a child process
Running 'opentabs plugin install slack' auto-expands to 'opentabs-plugin-slack' (convenience shorthand)
stdout and stderr from npm are piped to the terminal in real-time
On success, prints a success message with the installed package name
On npm failure (non-zero exit), prints a clear error message including the npm exit code
If the MCP server is running (check /health), calls POST /reload to trigger rediscovery after successful install
Server notification failure is non-fatal — prints a dim hint but does not fail the command
Help text includes examples: 'opentabs plugin install slack' and 'opentabs plugin install opentabs-plugin-slack'
userStories.notes:
Add the install subcommand in platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts under the existing pluginCmd. The implementation should:
1. Accept a single argument <name-or-package>
2. If the name doesn't start with 'opentabs-plugin-' or '@', prepend 'opentabs-plugin-'
3. Spawn `npm install -g <package>` using Bun.spawn with stdio: ['inherit', 'inherit', 'inherit']
4. After success, try to notify the server: read secret from config, fetch /health to check if server is running, if running then POST /reload with Bearer auth. Use the existing readConfig and getConfigPath from ../config.js.
5. The resolvePort helper should be used to determine the server port (requires passing optsWithGlobals through).

The command registration needs access to the global --port option. Follow the pattern from audit.ts where the action receives (args, command) and calls command.optsWithGlobals().

Also update the search handler output (line 70) to say: Install a plugin: opentabs plugin install <name>

userStories.title: Add opentabs plugin remove command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run 'opentabs plugin remove <name>' to uninstall a plugin cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'opentabs plugin remove opentabs-plugin-slack' executes 'npm uninstall -g opentabs-plugin-slack'
Running 'opentabs plugin remove slack' auto-expands to 'opentabs-plugin-slack' (same shorthand as install)
If the plugin path is in localPlugins in config.json, it is removed from the array and config is saved atomically
stdout and stderr from npm are piped to the terminal in real-time
On success, prints a success message
If the MCP server is running, calls POST /reload to trigger rediscovery after successful removal
userStories.notes:
Add in platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts. Similar pattern to install:
1. Accept a single argument <name-or-package>
2. Apply same name normalization (prepend 'opentabs-plugin-' if needed)
3. Spawn `npm uninstall -g <package>` with inherited stdio
4. After success, check if the plugin name or path appears in localPlugins in config — if so, remove it and save via atomicWriteConfig from ../config.js
5. Notify server via POST /reload if running

For the localPlugins cleanup: read config, filter out entries where the basename or full path matches the package name, write back if changed.

userStories.title: Add opentabs plugin list command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run 'opentabs plugin list' to see all installed plugins, even when the server is not running.
acceptanceCriteria: When server IS running: fetches /health and shows all discovered plugins (npm + local) with name, version, source, tab state, tool count
When server is NOT running: shows local plugins from config.json with path and basic info from their package.json/tools.json
When server is NOT running: attempts to scan global node_modules for npm plugins matching opentabs-plugin-* pattern and lists any found
Output format is clean and readable (similar to opentabs status plugin section)
userStories.notes:
Add in platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts. Two modes:

**Server running mode:**
- Try fetch /health (with 3s timeout). If successful, extract pluginDetails and failedPlugins from the response.
- Display similarly to status.ts plugin section but as the primary output.
- Use colorTabState from status.ts (already exported).

**Offline mode (server not running):**
- Read config via readConfig/getConfigPath to get localPlugins.
- For each local plugin: try to read package.json for name/version, try to read dist/tools.json for tool count.
- For npm plugins: use `npm list -g --json --depth=0` to find globally installed packages matching opentabs-plugin-* pattern. Parse the JSON output.
- Display with a note that tab state is only available when the server is running.

Import resolvePort from parse-port.js. Import isConnectionRefused from config.js.
